statement.p: A chef had \frac{2}{3} cups of flour and needed to use \frac{1}{8} cup to thicken a sauce. How much flour is left?

solution.p: We need to compute \frac{2}{3} - \frac{1}{8}\text{.} The denominators are 3 and 8\text{.} One common denominator is 24\text{,} so we move to rewrite each fraction using 24 as the denominator:
The numerical result is \frac{13}{24}\text{,} but a pure number does not answer this question. The amount of flour remaining is \frac{13}{24} cups.
